title: BATTLE OF THE ROCKETS 2026
what: Two competition rockets for 2026 Battle of the Rockets: Mars Rover — 6 in dia., 79 in, J540R-14, 1,000–2,000 ft, PETG-CF fins, rover payload; Altitude "Blue Phenix Jr" — F67-C, 6 tube fins, 801 ft AGL
PDR→CDR: iterated trapezoidal → elliptical → 3-fin PETG-CF (Mars Rover); J525-LW → J540R-14, T/W 5.97→6.85; 3→6 tube fins (Altitude); Blue Tube → phenolic; 2.19–2.25 cal
Ongoing Cₗ/Cᴅ/Cₘ database via rhoSimpleFoam RANS (Mach 0.1–0.7, α/β 0–10°); NASA TM X-195 drag model; EasyMini validation
how: Stability: SolidWorks CAD; 2.19–2.25 cal static margin via RockSim + MATLAB; Plastalina deadweight for CG; pre-competition test flights
CFD: rhoSimpleFoam RANS (Spalart-Allmaras, OpenFOAM v2412); snappyHexMesh Level 7 wake refinement; Mach/α/β sweeps; Python/Bash/SLURM on 16-core HPC
Fabrication: Blue Tube → phenolic after warping; 3 centering rings on motor mount; tube fins in aft-airframe slots; motor igniter resolved at competition
results: Altitude: 758 ft apogee — 5.4% from 801 ft; T/W 6.42:1; 22 ft/s descent (>20 ft/s limit); fully recovered
Mars Rover: 1,459 ft apogee (in spec; CFD 1,676 ft); T/W 6.11:1; 18.42 ft/s descent (≤20 ft/s); rover deployed and recovered
Cᴅ = 0.0227 (Altitude, viscous) / 0.0162 (Mars Rover); nose cone pressure drag identified as Altitude optimization target
5-person team, $1,400 budget; Culpeper VA competition (4/9–11); contributed to BNL publication
